accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context: EPA rule, CRA authority, Federal Register citation, and all dates are accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the description of chapter 8 of title 5 as the congressional review of agency rulemaking is accurate context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the primary purpose is nullifying the EPA Waste Emissions Charge rule via congressional disapproval.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Identifies the disapproval acts under chapter 8 of title 5 (CRA) to render the EPA rule without force or effect.

exceptions_conditions.why: The resolution contains no material exceptions or conditions; the summary correctly notes there are none.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the House (Feb. 26), Senate (Feb. 27), and approval (Mar. 14, 2025) dates and that no future deadlines are specified.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the essential operative effect: disapproving the rule so it has no force or effect.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized, internally consistent, and reads clearly.

concise.why: Appropriately concise with minimal padding for a brief resolution.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the EPA, the rule title, Public Law 119-2, and H.J. Res. 35.

correct_figures.why: The Federal Register citation (89 Fed. Reg. 91094), publication date, and legislative dates all match the bill text.
